result:
**Goal: Add a Mycorrhizal Network & Wood Wide Web simulation mode.**

Model the underground fungal highway that connects forest trees — mycorrhizal hyphae branching through soil, linking root systems into a shared communication and resource-trading network. Mother trees detect stressed seedlings and route carbon/phosphorus through the fungal mesh; pest-attacked trees broadcast chemical alarm signals that propagate through the network triggering defensive responses in neighbors; fungi take a carbon "tax" for brokering transfers. Visually: a split above-ground forest canopy over a below-ground web of pulsing hyphal connections with flowing nutrient particles, plus a network-topology view and time-series sparklines. Six presets spanning old-growth cooperation, drought triage, pest cascade, clear-cut fragmentation, monoculture vs. biodiversity, and seasonal nutrient cycling. This fills the identified soil science / fungal ecology gap, plays to the project's strengths in emergence and self-organization, and would look striking in the terminal.
